figures.caption: Figure 1: Human Perception Alignment. Accuracy (%) of various models evaluated on human perception annotations. ∗ indicates statistical significance (p < 0.05) compared to the 50% random baseline via binomial test.
Figure 2: Categorical Shift Tracking. The trajectory of accuracy across transformer layers for the base emotion2vec on MSPImprov. Shaded regions denote standard deviation.

Table 1: Categorical Emotion Evaluation. Triplet accuracy (%) under controlled adversarial settings. Values are Mean ± ±SD (dashes denote exclusion due to presence in pre-training corpora for zero-shot evaluation). Performance frequently falls to or below random chance (50 %), demonstrating a fundamental vulnerability to acoustic distractors.

Table 2: Shift Discriminability (Accuracy). Performance comparison across four datasets. Results are reported in Mean ± ±SD (%) (dashes denote exclusion due to presence in pre-training corpora for zero-shot evaluation). Accuracies remain near random chance (50 %), exposing the metric’s inability to track continuous emotion attributes.

Table 3: Trend Monotonicity Evaluation. Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) across datasets. Results are reported in Mean ±SD (dashes denote exclusion due to presence in pre-training corpora for zero-shot evaluation). Near-zero correlations show the metric lacks a monotonic relationship with emotional magnitude.
